<objective>
Bump `fsb-mcp-server` 0.8.0 -> 0.9.0 across the FOUR source-of-truth files in lockstep (per 258-CONTEXT.md decision "Version bump: in-tree only"), regenerate the compiled `mcp/build/version.js` via `cd mcp && npm run build`, and confirm `tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js` + the full `npm test` chain both exit 0. Plan 258-02 ships the in-tree bump only -- final `npm publish fsb-mcp-server@0.9.0` remains user-gated post-merge per the v0.9.60 / v0.9.61 precedent recorded in STATE.md "Pending User-Gated Actions".

Purpose:
- Encode the v0.9.62 milestone as a BREAKING release (0.8.0 -> 0.9.0 per semver) -- the major+minor bump signals to downstream MCP clients that the visual-session contract changed in an incompatible way.
- Lock the new version against future drift: the parity test asserts every metadata source matches canonicalVersion, so any subsequent plan that updates one source without the others fails CI.
- Land before the docs plan (258-03) so the CHANGELOG entry and mcp/README banner can reference a version that is actually in-tree, not speculative.

Output:
- 4 source-file edits (single literal substitution each, except server.json which has TWO occurrences in one file).
- 1 build step (`cd mcp && npm run build`) regenerates the TypeScript compilation output.
- 1 atomic commit landing all five file changes (4 sources + 1 rebuilt artifact) together. The build output IS checked in (verified by `git log --diff-filter=M -- mcp/build/version.js` showing prior bumps lived alongside source bumps).

OUT OF SCOPE for this plan:
- The two stub registrations + TOOL_REMOVED typed error (Plan 258-01 owns those).
- CHANGELOG entry + mcp/README banner (Plan 258-03 owns those).
- New parity-test assertions (the test already gates all four sources; no shape change is needed for this bump).
- `npm publish fsb-mcp-server@0.9.0` (user-gated post-merge).
</objective>

<interfaces>
Key contracts the executor needs verbatim. No codebase exploration required.

From mcp/src/version.ts (entire file -- 7 lines, single source of truth for runtime version):

```typescript
export const FSB_SERVER_NAME = 'fsb';
export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.8.0';
export const FSB_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT = 7225;
export const FSB_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_URL = `ws://localhost:${FSB_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT}`;
export const DEFAULT_HTTP_HOST = '127.0.0.1';
export const DEFAULT_HTTP_PORT = 7226;
export const FSB_REGISTRY_NAME = 'io.github.lakshmanturlapati/fsb-mcp-server';
```

Only LINE 2 changes (the FSB_MCP_VERSION literal). All other lines preserve byte-for-byte.

From mcp/package.json (lines 1-6, the relevant top of file -- only line 3 `version` changes):

```json
{
  "name": "fsb-mcp-server",
  "version": "0.8.0",
  "description": "FSB Browser Automation MCP Server",
  "license": "BUSL-1.1",
  "author": "Lakshman Turlapati",
```

The rest of the package.json (name, scripts, dependencies, etc.) preserves byte-for-byte.

From mcp/server.json (entire file -- 18 lines, TWO version occurrences -- both change):

```json
{
  "$schema": "https://static.modelcontextprotocol.io/schemas/2025-12-11/server.schema.json",
  "name": "io.github.lakshmanturlapati/fsb-mcp-server",
  "title": "FSB MCP Server",
  "description": "Control the FSB browser extension from any MCP client using a local stdio or Streamable HTTP companion runtime.",
  "version": "0.8.0",                                              // line 6 -- change to "0.9.0"
  "packages": [
    {
      "registryType": "npm",
      "identifier": "fsb-mcp-server",
      "version": "0.8.0",                                          // line 11 -- change to "0.9.0"
      "transport": {
        "type": "stdio"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

From tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js (lines 23-26 -- canonicalVersion is the test's single expected value):

```javascript
const repoRoot = path.resolve(__dirname, '..');
const canonicalVersion = '0.8.0';

function readText(relativePath) {
```

ONLY line 25 changes (canonicalVersion literal). The test logic, the readText / readJson helpers, the assertion functions, the CLI-output assertions, and the explicit README version-scan all stay byte-for-byte.

From tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js (lines 65-96, the test body the bump must pass):

```javascript
async function run() {
  const packageJson = readJson('mcp/package.json');
  const serverJson = readJson('mcp/server.json');
  const versionSource = readText('mcp/src/version.ts');
  const packageReadme = readText('mcp/README.md');
  const rootReadme = readText('README.md');

  console.log('\n--- metadata parity ---');
  assertEqual(packageJson.version, canonicalVersion, 'mcp/package.json version stays on canonical version parity target');
  assertEqual(extractRuntimeVersion(versionSource), canonicalVersion, 'FSB_MCP_VERSION matches canonical package version');
  assertEqual(serverJson.version, canonicalVersion, 'server.json top-level version matches canonical package version');
  assertEqual(serverJson.packages[0].version, canonicalVersion, 'server.json package version matches canonical package version');

  console.log('\n--- cli output parity ---');
  const helpOutput = runCommand('node mcp/build/index.js help');
  const installOutput = runCommand('node mcp/build/index.js install');
  assert(helpOutput.includes(`FSB MCP Server ${canonicalVersion}`), 'help output prints canonical MCP version');
  assert(installOutput.includes(`FSB MCP Server ${canonicalVersion}`), 'install output prints canonical MCP version');

  console.log('\n--- docs flow parity ---');
  assert(packageReadme.includes('doctor'), 'mcp README mentions doctor');
  assert(packageReadme.includes('status --watch'), 'mcp README mentions status --watch');
  assert(rootReadme.includes('doctor'), 'root README mentions doctor');
  assert(rootReadme.includes('status --watch'), 'root README mentions status --watch');

  const explicitVersions = [
    ...collectExplicitVersions(packageReadme),
    ...collectExplicitVersions(rootReadme),
  ];
  for (const version of explicitVersions) {
    assertEqual(version, canonicalVersion, `explicit README MCP version reference stays on ${canonicalVersion}`);
  }
}
```

The test calls `runCommand('node mcp/build/index.js help')` and `runCommand('node mcp/build/index.js install')` -- both read from the COMPILED `mcp/build/index.js` which imports `FSB_MCP_VERSION` from `mcp/build/version.js`. If the rebuild step is skipped, the build output still reads '0.8.0' and the CLI parity assertions FAIL even though the source files are at '0.9.0'. The rebuild is therefore non-optional.

The test ALSO scans explicit README references for `fsb-mcp-server@<version>` and `FSB MCP Server <version>` patterns (the `collectExplicitVersions` helper). If mcp/README.md or root README.md still contain `0.8.0` literals in those patterns, the test FAILS. Investigate before bumping:

```bash
grep -nE "fsb-mcp-server@0\\.8\\.0|FSB MCP Server 0\\.8\\.0" mcp/README.md README.md
```

If either file contains those literal patterns, those references MUST be bumped too (or this plan's scope expands to include the README edits). 258-CONTEXT.md says only 4 files in lockstep; the planner has verified the existing READMEs do NOT contain `fsb-mcp-server@0.8.0` or `FSB MCP Server 0.8.0` literals at the time of writing (the v0.7.4 / v0.8.0 sections refer to v0.8.0 prose-style, not the matched patterns). The executor MUST re-verify this with the grep above before the bump.

From mcp/build/version.js (compiled output -- regenerated by `npm run build`):

```javascript
export const FSB_SERVER_NAME = 'fsb';
export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.8.0';
// ... (5 more constant exports identical to source)
//# sourceMappingURL=version.js.map
```

After `cd mcp && npm run build`, line 2 reads `export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0';` and the sourceMappingURL comment is preserved.

From mcp/package.json scripts block (line 43):

```json
"build": "npm run clean && tsc && cp ../extension/ai/tool-definitions.js ai/tool-definitions.cjs",
```

The build command runs `tsc` (compiles all .ts under mcp/src/ to mcp/build/) AND copies the extension's tool-definitions.js to mcp/ai/tool-definitions.cjs (Phase 246 parity canary). Both steps run sequentially; the cp step does NOT affect version.ts compilation.

  <name>Task 1: Bump the four source-of-truth files to 0.9.0 in a single coordinated edit</name>

  <read_first>
    1. `.planning/phases/258-removal-migration-errors-package-0.9.0/258-CONTEXT.md` decisions block "Version bump: in-tree only" (the four-file lockstep table; verify against actual file contents before editing).
    2. `mcp/src/version.ts` (entire 7-line file -- only line 2 changes).
    3. `mcp/package.json` (line 3 is the version field; verify by re-reading at the time of edit because dependency-version bumps in unrelated PRs may have shifted line numbers).
    4. `mcp/server.json` (entire 18-line file -- two version occurrences at lines 6 and 11).
    5. `tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js` lines 1-50 (the canonicalVersion constant at line 25; verify the surrounding assertion block has not changed).

If either file has matches, surface this to the user BEFORE bumping -- the README content edits would expand this plan's scope.
  </read_first>

  <behavior>
    - Exactly four source files are edited; each edit is a single literal substitution from '0.8.0' to '0.9.0' EXCEPT mcp/server.json which substitutes TWO occurrences.
    - The edits are atomic: all four files are updated together so an interrupted execution cannot leave the tree in a half-bumped state.
    - The version.ts edit changes ONLY the FSB_MCP_VERSION literal on line 2; the other six exports (FSB_SERVER_NAME, FSB_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT, FSB_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_URL, DEFAULT_HTTP_HOST, DEFAULT_HTTP_PORT, FSB_REGISTRY_NAME) are preserved byte-for-byte.
    - The package.json edit changes ONLY the top-level `version` field; the name, scripts, dependencies, devDependencies, mcpName, repository, keywords, type, main, bin, files, engines, and license fields are preserved byte-for-byte.
    - The server.json edit changes BOTH version fields (top-level + packages[0]); the $schema, name, title, description, packages[].registryType, packages[].identifier, packages[].transport are preserved byte-for-byte.
    - The parity-test edit changes ONLY the canonicalVersion literal at line 25; all assertion logic, helper functions, and run() body are preserved byte-for-byte.
    - The build artifact `mcp/build/version.js` is NOT directly edited by this task. Task 2 runs the build to regenerate it.
    - The plan does NOT touch `mcp/CHANGELOG.md` or `mcp/README.md` -- those land in Plan 258-03.
  </behavior>

  <action>
    Run all four edits before proceeding to Task 2 (which builds and verifies). Use the Edit tool for each (a Read of each file MUST happen first per the Edit-tool contract -- `read_first` step 2-5 covers this).

    **Step 1.1 -- Bump mcp/src/version.ts line 2.**

    Single literal substitution:

    OLD: `export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.8.0';`
    NEW: `export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0';`

    Use the Edit tool with old_string=`export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.8.0';` and new_string=`export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0';`. The string is unique in the file (it appears exactly once on line 2).

    **Step 1.2 -- Bump mcp/package.json line 3.**

    Single literal substitution. The version field appears as the second key in the JSON object:

    OLD: `  "version": "0.8.0",`
    NEW: `  "version": "0.9.0",`

    Note the leading two spaces (JSON indentation) and the trailing comma. The string `"version": "0.8.0"` is unique at the top-level scope; lower in the file the keys-and-values use different indentation. If the substitution is ambiguous (the Edit tool returns "multiple matches"), use the more specific anchor:

    OLD: `  "name": "fsb-mcp-server",\n  "version": "0.8.0",\n  "description":`
    NEW: `  "name": "fsb-mcp-server",\n  "version": "0.9.0",\n  "description":`

    This anchors the substitution against the surrounding keys.

    **Step 1.3 -- Bump mcp/server.json TWO version fields.**

    server.json has TWO version occurrences. Edit BOTH:

    Substitution A (line 6, top-level version):

    OLD: `  "description": "Control the FSB browser extension from any MCP client using a local stdio or Streamable HTTP companion runtime.",\n  "version": "0.8.0",`
    NEW: `  "description": "Control the FSB browser extension from any MCP client using a local stdio or Streamable HTTP companion runtime.",\n  "version": "0.9.0",`

    Anchoring the substitution against the preceding `description` line ensures the substitution matches the top-level `version` and NOT the nested packages[0].version (the indentation is also different: top-level uses 2-space indent while nested uses 6-space).

    Substitution B (line 11, packages[0].version):

    OLD: `      "identifier": "fsb-mcp-server",\n      "version": "0.8.0",`
    NEW: `      "identifier": "fsb-mcp-server",\n      "version": "0.9.0",`

    Anchored against the preceding `identifier` line at the same 6-space indent level.

    Alternative single-pass substitution (use this if the tool supports per-occurrence replace_all): the Edit tool has a `replace_all` parameter; passing `replace_all=true` on the literal `"version": "0.8.0"` would replace both occurrences in one call. The two-step anchored approach above is the safer default because it guarantees the substitutions target the intended lines (the JSON has no other unintended matches, but explicit anchoring is preferred).

    **Step 1.4 -- Bump tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js line 25.**

    Single literal substitution:

    OLD: `const canonicalVersion = '0.8.0';`
    NEW: `const canonicalVersion = '0.9.0';`

    The string is unique in the file (appears exactly once on line 25).

    **Step 1.5 -- Re-grep to confirm zero stale '0.8.0' literals in the four files.**

    After all four edits land:

    ```bash
    grep -nE "0\\.8\\.0" mcp/src/version.ts mcp/package.json mcp/server.json tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js
    # Expect: zero matches (exit code 1 from grep is fine; no output is the success signal)
    ```

    If any match remains, the bump is incomplete -- re-read the offending file and fix the missed literal.

    **Step 1.6 -- Defer commit to the end of Task 2.**

    Do NOT commit yet. Task 2 runs `cd mcp && npm run build` which writes the regenerated `mcp/build/version.js`. A single atomic commit lands all five files (4 sources + 1 rebuilt artifact) together.
  </action>

  <acceptance_criteria>
    The following grep-verifiable assertions MUST hold after this task is complete (BEFORE Task 2 runs the build):

    1. mcp/src/version.ts line 2 reads `'0.9.0'`. Verified by `grep -n "FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0'" mcp/src/version.ts` returning exactly 1 match on line 2.

    2. mcp/package.json line 3 reads `"version": "0.9.0"`. Verified by reading the file with `cat mcp/package.json` and confirming the value of the top-level `version` key -- equivalently `node -e "console.log(require('./mcp/package.json').version)"` prints `0.9.0`.

    3. mcp/server.json has TWO `"version": "0.9.0"` occurrences. Verified by `grep -c "\"version\": \"0.9.0\"" mcp/server.json` returning exactly 2 (top-level + packages[0]).

    4. tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js line 25 reads `canonicalVersion = '0.9.0'`. Verified by `grep -n "canonicalVersion = '0.9.0'" tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js` returning exactly 1 match on line 25.

    5. Zero stale `0.8.0` literals remain in the four edited source files. Verified by `grep -c "0\\.8\\.0" mcp/src/version.ts mcp/package.json mcp/server.json tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js` returning 0 for each file (or grep exiting 1 with no output).

    6. Non-version content in each edited file is unchanged. Verified by:
       - mcp/src/version.ts: `grep -c "FSB_SERVER_NAME\\|FSB_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_PORT\\|FSB_EXTENSION_BRIDGE_URL\\|DEFAULT_HTTP_HOST\\|DEFAULT_HTTP_PORT\\|FSB_REGISTRY_NAME" mcp/src/version.ts` returns at least 6 (one per other constant).

    8. ASCII-only edits. No emojis. No em-dashes between sentences (use `--`).

<task type="auto" tdd="true">
  <name>Task 2: Rebuild the TypeScript output, run the parity test, run the full test chain, and commit all five files together</name>

  <files>

  <read_first>

The Phase 258 Plan 01 changes if not yet built into mcp/build/ (Plan 01 also writes to mcp/src/errors.ts and mcp/src/tools/visual-session.ts; Plan 01's verify step ran its own `cd mcp && npm run build` so those build outputs should already reflect TOOL_REMOVED; verify by `grep -c "TOOL_REMOVED" mcp/build/errors.js mcp/build/tools/visual-session.js` returning at least 3 total. If Plan 01 was committed without the build step, this task's rebuild also picks them up).

  <behavior>
    - `cd mcp && npm run build` runs the TypeScript compiler and regenerates ALL .js files under mcp/build/ from the current .ts sources under mcp/src/.
    - After the build, mcp/build/version.js line 2 reads `export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0';` (matching the source change from Task 1).
    - The build ALSO picks up any Plan 258-01 changes in mcp/src/errors.ts and mcp/src/tools/visual-session.ts that may not have been built and committed yet. The rebuild is idempotent if Plan 01 already built.
    - `tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js` exits 0: all four metadata-parity assertions pass (package.json, FSB_MCP_VERSION runtime, server.json top-level, server.json packages[0]), both CLI-output parity assertions pass (help prints `FSB MCP Server 0.9.0`, install prints `FSB MCP Server 0.9.0`), and the README explicit-version scan does NOT find any stale `0.8.0` pattern matches (verified separately in Task 1 Step 1.5 read_first).
    - `npm test` exits 0 against the new 0.9.0 build. No new tests are added; no test bodies change.
    - A single atomic commit lands all FIVE files: the four source bumps + mcp/build/version.js (and any other regenerated mcp/build/*.js files that the Task 1 source bumps + Plan 01 unbuilt sources touched).
    - The commit message MUST cite MIGRATION-05 and 258-02 plan reference.
    - `npm publish fsb-mcp-server@0.9.0` is NOT run -- per the 258-CONTEXT.md "Version bump: in-tree only" decision and the STATE.md "Pending User-Gated Actions" entry. The publish remains the user's manual post-merge step.
  </behavior>

  <action>

    **Step 2.1 -- Rebuild the TypeScript output.**

    This runs `npm run clean && tsc && cp ../extension/ai/tool-definitions.js ai/tool-definitions.cjs`. The expected stdout is the `clean` step's no-op then `tsc` exits silently on success, then `cp` exits silently on success.

    Expected exit code: 0. If `tsc` reports an error, surface it -- this most likely means Plan 258-01's stub conversion introduced a TypeScript issue and must be debugged BEFORE re-running the build. Do NOT proceed to commit.

    **Step 2.2 -- Confirm mcp/build/version.js is regenerated at 0.9.0.**

    ```bash
    grep -n "FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0'" mcp/build/version.js
    ```

    Expected: exactly 1 match on line 2 of the compiled output.

    Also confirm the sourceMappingURL comment is preserved (TypeScript writes it as the final line):

    ```bash

    ```

    Expected: `//# sourceMappingURL=version.js.map`.

    **Step 2.3 -- Run the targeted parity test.**

    ```bash
    node tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js
    ```

    Expected: exit code 0. Expected trailing line: `=== Results: N passed, 0 failed ===` where N >= 10 (4 metadata-parity + 2 CLI-output + 4 docs-flow assertions). If `failed > 0`, the test prints `FAIL:` lines naming the offending source; debug and fix.

    Common failure modes to expect (and how to fix):
    - "mcp/package.json version stays on canonical version parity target (expected: 0.9.0, got: 0.8.0)" -> Task 1 Step 1.2 missed the package.json edit.
    - "FSB_MCP_VERSION matches canonical package version (expected: 0.9.0, got: 0.8.0)" -> Task 1 Step 1.1 missed the version.ts edit.
    - "server.json top-level version matches canonical package version" or "server.json package version matches canonical package version" -> Task 1 Step 1.3 missed one of the two server.json substitutions.
    - "help output prints canonical MCP version" or "install output prints canonical MCP version" -> Step 2.1 rebuild was skipped or failed. Re-run `cd mcp && npm run build`.
    - "explicit README MCP version reference stays on 0.9.0" -> The READMEs DO contain `fsb-mcp-server@0.8.0` or `FSB MCP Server 0.8.0` patterns. Task 1 Step `read_first` 6 was supposed to surface this. If the test fires, ABORT and surface to the user: this is a scope expansion (READMEs need editing).

    **Step 2.4 -- Run the full test chain.**

    ```bash
    npm test
    ```

    Expected: exit code 0. The full chain runs ~60+ test files including the parity test (which now passes at 0.9.0) and the visual-session tests (which Plan 258-01 has already adjusted to expect the TOOL_REMOVED stub behavior, OR which were retired in Phase 256/257 and no longer reference start_visual_session / end_visual_session directly).

    If `npm test` fails, the failures are most likely:
    - Pre-existing tests still calling start_visual_session / end_visual_session expecting v0.9.36 behavior. Phase 256/257 should have retired those; if any remain, Plan 258-01's stub conversion exposes them -- surface to the user as a regression (planner expected these to be retired already; if not, Plan 259 owns the broader test rewrites and this plan should still pass without that work).
    - tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js -- handled in Step 2.3 above.
    - tests/mcp-visual-session-contract.test.js -- this test predates the v0.9.62 work and may still reference start_visual_session by name. Investigate: `grep -c "start_visual_session\\|end_visual_session" tests/mcp-visual-session-contract.test.js`. If it references them in assertions about live behavior, the stub conversion may have broken those assertions. Surface to the user -- this is a Plan 259 concern (TEST-01 rewrite) and should not block 258-02.

    **Step 2.5 -- Stage all five files and commit.**

    ```bash
    git add mcp/src/version.ts mcp/package.json mcp/server.json tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js mcp/build/version.js
    ```

    Also stage any other regenerated mcp/build/*.js files the rebuild produced (typically mcp/build/errors.js + mcp/build/tools/visual-session.js if Plan 258-01's source changes had not been built and committed yet -- check with `git status mcp/build/`).


    DO NOT run `npm publish` -- the publish remains user-gated post-merge per STATE.md "Pending User-Gated Actions".

<threat_model>
## Trust Boundaries

| Boundary | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Source-of-truth files -> compiled build output | mcp/src/version.ts is the canonical source; mcp/build/version.js is its TypeScript-compiled copy. Drift between the two is the failure mode the parity test catches. |
| Local repo -> npm registry | The publish step (out of scope for this plan) crosses this boundary. The in-tree bump is bounded to the local working tree; no network I/O happens in this plan. |
| Test runner -> mcp CLI subprocess | tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js spawns `node mcp/build/index.js help` and `node mcp/build/index.js install` via execSync. The subprocesses read their version from mcp/build/version.js; the parity test asserts the printed output matches canonicalVersion. |

## STRIDE Threat Register

Plan 258-02 changes only metadata (version strings) and runs the build + tests. No new behavior, no new attack surface.

| Threat ID | Category | Component | Disposition | Mitigation Plan |
|-----------|----------|-----------|-------------|-----------------|
| T-258-02-01 | Tampering | A future plan bumps mcp/src/version.ts without updating mcp/package.json or mcp/server.json, leaving the published npm package version mismatched with the runtime version | mitigate | tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js is in the npm test chain (root package.json line 16 includes `node tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js`). Any drift across the four sources fails the test, which fails the `ci / all-green` PR gate. The parity test is the ONLY enforcement layer for version coherence; this plan preserves it (only bumps canonicalVersion). |
| T-258-02-02 | Tampering | A future plan edits mcp/build/version.js directly without updating mcp/src/version.ts, so the compiled output diverges from source | mitigate | The build script (`tsc`) regenerates mcp/build/version.js from source on every `npm run build`. Any direct edit to mcp/build/version.js is overwritten on the next build. The npm test chain calls `npm --prefix mcp run build` before the parity test (root package.json line 16: `... && npm --prefix mcp run build && ...`), so direct edits to build output never survive the test gate. |
| T-258-02-03 | Information disclosure | The new 0.9.0 version is announced via the compiled CLI output (`node mcp/build/index.js help` prints `FSB MCP Server 0.9.0`) | accept | The version is public information by design -- it appears in npm metadata, in the MCP catalog (via server.json), and in any client that calls tools/list. No PII, no auth tokens, no internal identifiers leak. |
| T-258-02-04 | Denial of service | A pre-publish accidental `npm publish` would push a 0.9.0 release before the user is ready, blocking the user from making last-minute changes without a 0.9.1 follow-up | mitigate | The plan explicitly forbids `npm publish` (Step 2.5 deferral + Acceptance Criteria #10). The publish is user-gated per STATE.md "Pending User-Gated Actions" and 258-CONTEXT.md "Version bump: in-tree only" decisions. There is no automation in this plan that runs `npm publish`. |
| T-258-02-05 | Tampering | The README explicit-version scan in the parity test (lines 91-95) could miss a stale 0.8.0 reference in a future README change, allowing version drift | accept | The scan uses two patterns: `fsb-mcp-server@(\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)` and `FSB MCP Server (\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)`. Both patterns are anchored against the canonical-version literal forms; the test's `assertEqual(version, canonicalVersion, ...)` catches mismatches. If a future plan introduces a NEW pattern (e.g. `v0.8.0` shorthand), the test would miss it -- but extending the scan to cover new patterns is out of scope for this plan and properly belongs in a parity-test enhancement plan (deferred). Current posture: the existing two patterns are sufficient because the v0.7.4 / v0.8.0 README sections use the matched forms exclusively. |
| T-258-02-06 | Elevation of privilege | The build step (`cd mcp && npm run build`) writes to mcp/build/, mcp/ai/tool-definitions.cjs, and consumes ../extension/ai/tool-definitions.js. A symlink or path-traversal attack via a manipulated package.json `build` script could exfiltrate files | accept | The build script is committed in mcp/package.json line 43: `npm run clean && tsc && cp ../extension/ai/tool-definitions.js ai/tool-definitions.cjs`. The cp source path is fixed (`../extension/ai/tool-definitions.js`); a path traversal would require modifying mcp/package.json itself, which would be caught in code review. No symlink or absolute-path injection is possible from THIS plan's edits (the plan only changes version literals, not paths or scripts). |

**Verdict: CLEARED.** Plan 258-02 changes only metadata; the existing parity-test gate enforces coherence; the publish step is explicitly user-gated and not automated. No new attack surface.
</threat_model>

<verification>
After both tasks complete, verify the full integration end-to-end:

**Static checks (run after Task 1):**

```bash
# All four sources at 0.9.0
grep -c "FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0'" mcp/src/version.ts
# Expect: 1

node -e "console.log(require('./mcp/package.json').version)"
# Expect: 0.9.0

node -e "const s = require('./mcp/server.json'); console.log(s.version, s.packages[0].version)"
# Expect: 0.9.0 0.9.0

grep -c "canonicalVersion = '0.9.0'" tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js
# Expect: 1

# Zero stale 0.8.0 literals across the four source files
grep -c "0\\.8\\.0" mcp/src/version.ts mcp/package.json mcp/server.json tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js
# Expect: 0 per file (or grep exits 1 with no output)
```

**Build + behaviour checks (run after Task 2):**

```bash
# Compiled output regenerated at 0.9.0
grep -c "FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0'" mcp/build/version.js
# Expect: 1

# Parity test green
node tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js
# Expect: exit 0; trailing line "=== Results: N passed, 0 failed ===" with N >= 10

# CLI prints 0.9.0
node mcp/build/index.js help | head -1
# Expect: literal "FSB MCP Server 0.9.0"

# Full chain green
npm test
# Expect: exit 0; zero "FAIL:" lines

# Commit landed all five files atomically
git log -1 --name-only | grep -E "mcp/src/version.ts|mcp/package.json|mcp/server.json|tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js|mcp/build/version.js" | wc -l
# Expect: 5 (or 6+ if Plan 258-01's mcp/build outputs were also picked up by this build)

# Commit message cites MIGRATION-05 + 258-02
git log -1 --pretty=%B | grep -c "MIGRATION-05"
# Expect: at least 1

git log -1 --pretty=%B | grep -c "258-02"
# Expect: at least 1
```

**Negative invariants (must NOT hold):**

```bash
# No npm publish in shell history
fc -l -n 1 | grep -c "npm publish"
# Expect: 0 (best-effort; shell history depends on session)

# No stale 0.8.0 in build output
grep -c "0\\.8\\.0" mcp/build/version.js
# Expect: 0
```
</verification>

3. mcp/server.json top-level version field AND packages[0].version both read `"version": "0.9.0"`.
4. tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js line 25 reads `const canonicalVersion = '0.9.0';`.
5. `cd mcp && npm run build` exits 0 and regenerates mcp/build/version.js so its line 2 reads `export const FSB_MCP_VERSION = '0.9.0';`.
6. `node tests/mcp-version-parity.test.js` exits 0 with all 10+ assertions passing (4 metadata-parity, 2 CLI-output, 4 docs-flow).
7. `node mcp/build/index.js help` prints `FSB MCP Server 0.9.0` on the first line.
8. `npm test` exits 0 with no regressions.
9. A single atomic commit lands all 5 files (4 sources + mcp/build/version.js; possibly 6+ if Plan 258-01 unbuilt sources are picked up by this build).
10. The commit message cites MIGRATION-05 and 258-02.
11. `npm publish fsb-mcp-server@0.9.0` is NOT executed (user-gated post-merge per STATE.md and 258-CONTEXT.md).
12. ASCII-only commit message and edits. No emojis. No em-dashes between sentences (use `--`).
</success_criteria>
